records.morph: [imp. Forewent 2; p. p. Foregone (?; 115); p. pr. & vb. n. Foregoing.]

defs: To quit; to relinquish; to leave.
quotes: Stay at the third cup, or forego the place.

defs: To relinquish the enjoyment or advantage of; to give up; to resign; to renounce; -- said of a thing already enjoyed, or of one within reach, or anticipated.
quotes: All my patrimony,, If need be, I am ready to forego.
Thy lovers must their promised heaven forego.
[He] never forewent an opportunity of honest profit.
notes: ☞ Forgo is the better spelling etymologically, but the word has been confused with Forego, to go before.

records.ety: [AS. foregān; fore + gān to go; akin to G. vorgehen to go before, precede. See Go, v. i.]

defs: To go before; to precede; -- used especially in the present and past participles.
quotes: Pleasing remembrance of a thought foregone.
For which the very mother's face forewent The mother's special patience.
